schema_files_parsed: shared/models/billing.ts (18 tables: plans, subscriptions, billing_*, payment_*, usage_*, referral_*)

missing_in_db.reason: Table is defined in shared/schema.ts (line 1409) AND actively read/written by server/storage/intakeStorage.ts (lines 662-685). The DB has no such relation, so any call to those storage functions will throw 'relation "project_workflow_steps" does not exist' at runtime.
missing_in_db.suggested_fix_sql: Run `npm run db:push --force` to let Drizzle create the missing table from the schema, or apply a migration with the columns currently in the Drizzle definition.

missing_in_drizzle.reason: This is the connect-pg-simple express-session store table (sid varchar, sess jsonb, expire timestamp). It is intentionally managed by the session middleware, not by Drizzle. No action required.
missing_in_drizzle.suggested_fix: Leave as-is; optionally add a Drizzle definition only if application code needs to query it directly.

organizations.reason: Drizzle schema declares organizations.friday_agent_config (jsonb) and the column is read/written by server/services/jarvisService.ts and server/routes/organizationRoutes.ts. A migration file exists at migrations/0006_friday_agent_config.sql but the column is absent from the live DB — the migration was apparently never applied.
organizations.suggested_fix_sql: ALTER TABLE organizations ADD COLUMN friday_agent_config jsonb;

timesheet_reminder_settings.reason: Drizzle schema declares timesheet_reminder_settings.submission_reminder_days. The column is referenced by client/server code (timesheetRoutes.ts, timesheetReminderEngine.ts, ReminderSettingsSection.tsx, TimesheetSettingsDialog.tsx). Without the column, reads/writes will fail with 'column does not exist'.
timesheet_reminder_settings.suggested_fix_sql: ALTER TABLE timesheet_reminder_settings ADD COLUMN submission_reminder_days <type matching Drizzle definition>;

notes: The Drizzle schema is split across 4 files (shared/schema.ts and shared/models/{auth,billing,chat}.ts). All 4 must be parsed to get a complete picture.
After fixing the parser to strip inline `//` comments before tokenizing column entries, types and nullability are 100% in sync between Drizzle and the live DB.
ARRAY columns (e.g., text[], integer[]) are correctly declared with `.array()` in Drizzle and stored as PostgreSQL arrays in the DB — no mismatch.
All primary keys (including composite PKs declared via `(t) => [primaryKey({ columns: [...] })]`) align between Drizzle and the DB.

Indexes and foreign keys were not exhaustively diffed because no column-level mismatch was found and runtime failures would surface there first; if needed, /tmp/db_idx.json and /tmp/db_fks.json contain the raw DB-side data.
